---
title: "4.1.2 Operating systems"
subtitle: "Management functions · P157-P159"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)

## Memory Management

### Managing RAM and storage

<span class="term">Memory management</span> carries out the following functions:

::: {.tight-list}
- manages primary storage, RAM
- allows data to be moved between RAM and HDD/SSD during program execution
- keeps track of all memory locations
- carries out memory protection
:::

Memory protection helps ensure that two competing applications cannot use the same memory locations at the same time.

## Memory Management

### Why memory protection matters

If memory protection was not done:

::: {.tight-list}
- data would probably be lost
- applications could produce incorrect results
- security issues could occur if data is placed in the wrong location
- in extreme cases, the computer could crash
:::

## Security Management

### Data integrity, confidentiality and availability

The function of <span class="term">security management</span> is to ensure the integrity, confidentiality and availability of data.

This can be achieved by:

::: {.tight-list}
- carrying out operating system updates
- keeping anti-virus and other security software up to date
- communicating with a firewall to check traffic to and from the computer
:::

## Security Management

### Privileges and recovery

Security management can also:

::: {.tight-list}
- use privileges to prevent users entering private areas
- set up user accounts, passwords and user IDs
- maintain access rights for all users
- offer data recovery and system restore
- help prevent illegal intrusion into the computer system
:::

## Hardware Peripheral Management

### Device drivers and resource priority

<span class="term">Hardware management</span> involves all input and output peripheral devices.

Hardware management:

::: {.tight-list}
- communicates with devices using <span class="term">device drivers</span>
- translates data into a format the device can understand
- ensures each hardware resource has a priority
- manages input/output devices using queues and buffers
:::

## Printer Management Example

### Queues, buffers and interrupts

When printing a document:

::: {.tight-list}
- the printer driver is located and loaded into memory
- data is sent to a printer buffer
- if the printer is busy, data is sent to a printer queue
- control commands are sent during the printing process
- error messages and interrupts from the printer are handled
:::

## File Management

### Main tasks

The main tasks of <span class="term">file management</span> include:

::: {.tight-list}
- file naming conventions, such as filename.docx
- create, open, close, delete, rename, copy and move
- maintaining directory structures
- maintaining access control mechanisms
- allocating memory by reading a file from HDD/SSD and loading it into memory
:::

## Interrupts and Application Platform

### Linked OS functions

The textbook links two operating system functions to later sections:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">Interrupts</span> — see Section 4.1.4
- <span class="term">Platform for running of application software</span> — see Section 4.1.3
:::

Both are part of the operating system function list in Figure 4.7.

## Classroom Check

### Explain OS management functions

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- memory management tracks locations and protects RAM
- security management protects integrity, confidentiality and availability
- hardware management uses device drivers, queues and buffers
- printer management may use buffers, queues, commands, errors and interrupts
- file management handles names, operations, directory structures and access control
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Multitasking and user accounts

The next deck completes the operating system functions.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)
